topic: r6g Family of AWS Instance Types

  entities:

    AWS|Company
    Graviton|Product
    Graviton2|Product
    r6g family|Product
    r6g.large|Product
    r6g.16xlarge|Product
    Intel|Company
    AMD|Company

  proposition: AWS has developed its own ARM-based processor called Graviton.

    entity-entity relationships:
    AWS|DEVELOPED|Graviton

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton|ARCHITECTURE|ARM-based
    Graviton|TYPE|processor

  proposition: Graviton is designed to deliver better price/performance than Intel and AMD equivalent processors.

    entity-entity relationships:
    Graviton|COMPARED_TO|Intel
    Graviton|COMPARED_TO|AMD

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton|DESIGN_GOAL|better price/performance than Intel and AMD equivalent processors

  proposition: The `r6g` family of instance types uses the Graviton2 processor.

    entity-entity relationships:
    r6g family|USES|Graviton2

    entity-attributes:
    r6g family|TYPE|instance types

  proposition: The Graviton2 processor offers 10-20% better performance for OLTP-style (constrained) graph queries.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton2|PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENT|10-20% better for OLTP-style (constrained) graph queries

  proposition: Larger, OLAP-ish queries may be slightly less performant with the Graviton2 processors than with Intel processors.

    entity-entity relationships:
    Graviton2|COMPARED_TO|Intel

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton2|PERFORMANCE_CHARACTERISTIC|slightly less performant for larger OLAP-ish queries than Intel processors

  proposition: The reduced performance of Graviton2 processors for OLAP-ish queries is owing to slightly less performant memory-paging performance.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton2|PERFORMANCE_LIMITATION|slightly less performant memory-paging performance for OLAP-ish queries

  proposition: The `r6g` family has a single-socket architecture.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    r6g family|ARCHITECTURE|single-socket

  proposition: The single-socket architecture of the `r6g` family means that performance scales linearly with compute capacity.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    r6g family|PERFORMANCE_CHARACTERISTIC|performance scales linearly with compute capacity

  proposition: Performance in the `r6g` family scales linearly from an `r6g.large` to an `r6g.16xlarge`.

    entity-entity relationships:
    r6g family|INCLUDES|r6g.large
    r6g family|INCLUDES|r6g.16xlarge

    entity-attributes:
    r6g family|PERFORMANCE_SCALING|linear from r6g.large to r6g.16xlarge

  proposition: The `r6g.16xlarge` is the largest instance type in the `r6g` family.

    entity-entity relationships:
    r6g.16xlarge|PART_OF|r6g family

    entity-attributes:
    r6g.16xlarge|SIZE_RANK|largest in the r6g family
